texto: When Benedict dies, he will have the pleasure of standing before whatever furious God he believes in, to answer for how it was that he knew for undeniable fact that one -- if not dozens -- of his priests repeatedly molested, abused and/or raped young children for decades, and he did nothing to stop it. How much does God believe the pope's argument that Vatican PR trumps pedophilia? Joe Ratzinger, 82, will soon find out.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Mark Morford que provoca temas ligados a familia. Ao questionar “When Benedict dies, he will have the pleasure of standing before whatever furious God he believes…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, catholic, catholicism, com foco em catholicism e child-abuse.

frase_original: When Benedict dies, he will have the pleasure of standing before whatever furious God he believes in, to answer for how it was that he knew for undeniable fact that one -- if not dozens -- of his priests repeatedly molested, abused and/or raped young children for decades, and he did nothing to stop it. How much does God believe the pope's argument that Vatican PR trumps pedophilia? Joe Ratzinger, 82, will soon find out.

texto: If there is a true measure of a person's soul, if there is a single gauge of real divinity, of how beautifully a fellow human honors this life, has genuine spiritual fire and is full of honest love and compassion, it has to be right there, in the eyes.The Dalai Lama's eyes sparkle and dance with laughter and unbridled love. The Pope's eyes are dark and glazed, bleak as obsidian marbles. Pat Robertson's eyes are rheumy and hollow, like tiny potholes of old wax. Goldman Sachs cretins, well, they don't use their own eyes at all; they just steal someone else's.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Mark Morford: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “If there is a true measure of a person's soul, if there is a single gauge of real divinity, of ho…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que espiritualidade, catholic, catholicism pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e catholicism.

The Pope's eyes are dark and glazed, bleak as obsidian marbles. Pat Robertson's eyes are rheumy and hollow, like tiny potholes of old wax. Goldman Sachs cretins, well, they don't use their own eyes at all; they just steal someone else's.

texto: It is, after all, far too easy to pinch and kick the bizarre Mormon Church; to say it's ripe for satire and parody is to say a Catholic schoolgirl is ripe for debauchery. It's like shooting polygamist fish in a barrel of coffee.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Mark Morford: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a educacao. O trecho “It is, after all, far too easy to pinch and kick the bizarre Mormon Church; to say it's ripe for …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que educacao, catholicism, child-abuse pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de child-abuse e mormonism.
